scenarios.title: Current stored digest round-trips through the canonical reader

gherkin.given: Disposable PostgreSQL contains a current non-empty approximately 380-word digest in the operator-owned global corpus
gherkin.when: digest.Generator.GetLatest reads the row for an authorized reader
gherkin.then: The exact stored prose, digest date, creation instant, quiet flag, word count, and model metadata return with no empty-state substitution

linkedTests.testId: existing canonical GetLatest typed-read canary to extend
existing real digest read integration canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: current stored digest marker and intended calendar date reach the real authenticated page

scenarios.title: Date boundary scans and formats correctly

gherkin.given: PostgreSQL stores digest_date as DATE and created_at as TIMESTAMPTZ at a timezone boundary
gherkin.when: The canonical reader scans the row
gherkin.then: Both values use compatible typed time values and the calendar date remains the intended database date without viewer or host timezone substitution

linkedTests.testId: existing typed DATE/TIMESTAMPTZ scan canary to extend
existing real digest read integration canary to preserve

scenarios.title: Query or scan failure is explicit

gherkin.given: The real reader reaches an owned query, scan, decode, or connection fault boundary
gherkin.then: Wrapped pgx.ErrNoRows alone represents absence and every other fault retains a typed error with no digest-derived fallback fields

linkedTests.testId: existing reader error-classification canary to extend
existing real Digest endpoint canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: reader failure never renders first-use copy or a substituted current date

gherkin.given: The authorized canonical reader completes successfully with no digest rows
gherkin.then: The first-use never-generated state shows with no Retry or sample prose and no failure, current, quiet, or stale content

linkedTests.testId: existing Digest page canary to extend
existing real Digest endpoint canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: real Digest page distinguishes empty quiet stale error session-ended and access-denied without leakage

gherkin.given: The latest successful row is intentionally quiet with valid persisted metadata
gherkin.then: Quiet day plus its persisted date and generation time are visible and no empty or read-error copy is present

linkedTests.testId: existing Digest projection canary to extend
existing real quiet-digest canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: quiet digest renders quiet day and persisted metadata without empty or error copy

scenarios.title: Stale digest is degraded not empty

gherkin.given: The latest successful row exceeds the explicit stale-age SST contract
gherkin.then: Stored prose, last-success metadata, and exact age remain visible under Degraded while Current and both empty states are absent

linkedTests.testId: existing Digest projection canary to extend
existing real Digest endpoint canary to extend

plannedTests.plannedTestId: stale digest shows Degraded age and stored prose and never Current or empty

gherkin.given: The session is missing, expired, or the authenticated identity lacks the explicit Digest read grant
gherkin.then: The router presents re-authentication or access denial and the response and accessibility tree contain no digest prose, date, source title, count, or existence signal

linkedTests.testId: existing Digest auth-projection canary to extend
existing server-rendered auth canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: unauthorized or ungranted Digest read removes all protected nodes from DOM and accessibility tree

scenarios.title: Digest states are accessible and responsive

gherkin.given: A real authenticated keyboard or screen-reader user at 320 CSS pixels, 200 percent zoom, and reduced motion
gherkin.then: Headings, semantic dates, prose, source links, status, and retry remain perceivable and operable without overlap, and protected nodes are removed on unauthorized outcomes

linkedTests.testId: existing Digest markup canary to extend
existing authenticated browser journey canary to preserve

plannedTests.plannedTestId: real Digest states expose exact typed dates truthful content privacy and accessible recovery
all real Digest states remain mutually exclusive announced and non-overlapping

scenarios.title: Stale digest has an explicit age contract

gherkin.given: Digest has a configured generation cadence and no hidden stale threshold
gherkin.then: One required positive SST value defines stale age for every reader and renderer and missing or invalid freshness configuration fails startup rather than defaulting

linkedTests.testId: existing config validation canary to extend for the required freshness key

scenarios.title: Digest reads follow the ratified global-corpus grant contract

gherkin.given: Active storage contains one operator-owned global latest digest and no auth_user_id column
gherkin.when: The ratified authorization model is applied
gherkin.then: Requirements, design, storage, generation, reads, and tests name one operator-owned global corpus gated by digest:read and digest:generate grants, and no scope certifies tenant or per-user row isolation because the digests table has no ownership column

linkedTests.testId: existing global-corpus canonical-read canary to preserve
existing Digest page authorization canary to extend
